title: The One With The Late Thanksgiving

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock & Wendy Knoller
Final check by Kim
Central Perk. Monica and Chandler enter, the others are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We need to talk to you about something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. We don't feel like we can host Thanksgiving this year.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you kidding?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's just with uhm, work and the stress of adoption, we just don't feel like we have the energy. Plus, we don't think it's fair that every year the burden falls on us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (skeptical) That doesn't sound like you... That's Monica talking!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no! We made this decision together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She's putting words in your mouth!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't you put words in people's mouths, you put turkey in people's mouths!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe this! This is Emma's first Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it's not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not? (whispering to Ross) When was she born?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, personally I think it's great you're giving yourself a break.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure. It's just as well... I mean, last year wasn't very good. I think she's losing her touch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? You are way off, lady!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Am I? Really? Am I? Well, why don't you cook Thanksgiving dinner and prove me wrong! Well, think about it, think about it, you'll be trying to top than you did last year. You'd be in competition... with yourself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's my favourite kind! Okay, we are doing this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't let yourself get manipulated this way!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, stay out of this, Chandler! This is between me... and ME!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are supposed to make these decisions together! Did you not watch the Doctor Phil I taped for you?
OPENING CREDITS
Joey and Rachel's apartment. Rachel and Monica are in there, and Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, happy needless-turkey-murder day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You guys, I ordered some chocolate pies from that bakery on Bleecker. Could you pick them up for me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're not making the pies yourself?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, no, I don't make chocolate pies. When I was younger I-I enter in this pie-eating contest. I ate so many that just the thought of them made me sick.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you at least win the contest?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 2 minutes, 12 pies and a part of one tin! Okay, I see you guys at 4.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can't wait!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This dinner is gonna be so great! In your face, last year "me"!
Monica leaves

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's Emma doing today?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, let's see... uh... I know that she has a meeting with her lawyer and then she has to make a very big poop. Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I wanna enter her in a baby beauty pageant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! That's the creepiest thing I've ever heard!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, but, well, before you say no, my friend Susanne is entering her kid and compared to Emma she's a real dog!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Phoebe, all babies are beautiful!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh... okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, just the idea of pitting one baby against another, I mean, you know, and judging who's cuter just for a trophy...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And a thousand dollars.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...is something I'm very interested in! Oh please, do not tell Ross. He still believes that (in a deep voice, mocking Ross) what's in the inside is important...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, oh, and Emma needs a cowgirl outfit for the competition.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where am I gonna get a cowgirl outfit on Thanksgiving?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I was thinking...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, take the clothes of Joey's Cabbage Patch Kid.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Chandler is watching TV and Monica is cooking

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Chandler's laughing) Did someone drop the baton again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why come all the way from Kansas to do that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (she tastes what she has cooked) I don't get older. I just get better!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what just occurred to me? This could be our last Thanksgiving just the two of us. I mean, we could be getting a baby soon!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't know that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Somebody is gonna pick us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but we haven't heard a thing from the adoption agency and it has been weeks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm telling ya! It's gonna happen. Next year it's gonna be you, me and the little Hemingway Bing. (pause) What, he's my favourite author!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Name one of his books.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (after a long pause) "The Firm"?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, let's see... uhm, okay, the turkey is in the oven, the stuffing is ready...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, you always cook this meal all by yourself. Let me help this year.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, Chandler, that's sweet. But you don't have to do everything Doctor Phil tells you to do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm serious, let me do something, just not the turkey or the stuffing, nothing "high profile".

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, let's see... Oh, the cranberry sauce, it is easy to make and no-one really cares about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell me more.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna go check on something across the hall. You start by washing these (she gives Chandler a bowl with cranberries. Then, while she's going outside, she sees him with a bottle of soap in his hands) Not with soap!! (she leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You obviously haven't tasted my Palmolive potatoes!
Ross and Joey enter

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Hey, guess what Joey has!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Three tickets to today's Rangers game!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, I wanted him to guess.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, they're great seats too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guess where they are?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Center ice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did I do something to you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hmm, the game's at one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dinner is at four, we'll never gonna make it back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So we'll leave before it's over, we'll be back in time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You say that now, but it could take us a long time to get back home. Plus Joey could get lost and and they could have to page us to go pick him up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, two times that happened!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, Monica has been working hard all day, she didn't wanna host this thing in the first place, we shouldn't go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's right, man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, I guess. Alright, so see you at four.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (pause) And get ready to taste my very special cranberries. Or should I say... chanberries!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's some gentle comedy, dude. (he and Ross leave)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We're still going at the game, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
Baby beauty contest

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: This is contestant number sixteen, Rebecca...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Phoebe, listen. Well, I think we gotta go. This place is really freaking me out. I've been watching this guy over there, I don't think he came with a kid!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We can't leave now! There was this one baby, Haley, who was favourite to win and she got croup, so she had to stay home! This competition just blew wide open, folks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, I think... It's just too weird, I just saw a one year old running around with pantyhose on!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I know, we should have been more prepared. (she looks around) It's okay. Now, the way I see it, our real competition now is Cameron. Oh my God, they just took her sweater off. Look at those arms! Hello Michelin Man.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Phoebe! Come on! You know what, it's already three o'clock and they haven't even gotten to Emma's group yet. We gotta go, we got dinner!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (panicking) But Emma's got what it takes, she could go all the way!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, you have to calm down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Rachel, the hottest babies in the Tri-State Area are in this room right now! I overheard one of the judges say that not one of them holds a candle to Emma!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You heard them say that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, okay. Alright, let's give to these babies something to cry about!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good! Oh yay! Let's get down to business! Emma needs some makeup!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, she's gonna look all washed out next to the other contestants!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Phoebe, I am not letting you put makeup on my baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because I already did!
Madison Square Garden
there's a lot of shouting and yelling

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, Bob, get off the guy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! What a game, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe Chandler is missing this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I am sorry he's not here too, but I got to say, (takes some nachos from a plate on the seat where Chandler should have been) I am really enjoying Nacho Chair.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'd probably enjoy it more if you didn't keep batting my hand away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhhh! These seats are great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I know! When I was here for Holidays on Ice (Joey looks around worried hoping no one heard that) I was sitting so far away Michelle Kwan couldn't read my banner!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks at the time) Wow, hey, we'd better get going. If we don't leave right now, we'll be late for dinner.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but it's a kind game! So we're a little late, you know, the girls will be there, let's stay just for one more goal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One more fight!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.
Chandler and Monica's apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where is everybody? They're forty-five minutes late!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe they are not here! I slave and I slave for what? They've ruined cranberry day!
The corridor. Ross and Joey have just arrived.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How late are we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Forty-five minutes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow (He opens his apartment door and throws their stuff in.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (handing him his coat) Here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Rachel and Phoebe are already there, okay? So they probably started without us. We could just slip in and no-one needs to know where we were! (he raises his hands and on his right one there's a Rangers foam finger)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You may want to lose the foam finger!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no, no, no. You just want to put it on your hand!
Rachel, Phoebe and Emma arrive

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You are not at Thanksgiving?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, we're late!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you doing here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We're late too! (Rachel screams)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We figured we could be late because you guys were gonna be on time (he points the foam finger at the girls)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't point that thing at me, Tribbiani!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, nobody's here? Monica's gonna kill us!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, where were you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, what's with the trophy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, we were at the Spelling Bee!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I won!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You won an adult Thanksgiving day spelling bee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! (spells it) Y-E-S. Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me see this... (he takes the trophy from Rachel's hands) Grand Supreme Little Darling, New York Division.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. That's me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You entered Emma into a Beauty Pageant?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And it looks like she put makeup on her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait a second, wait a second, where have I seen that cowgirl outfit before...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this, she's our daughter! That you would treat her like some kind of showdog is inexcusable!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She won a thousand dollars!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So this is an annual thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (realizing) Oh! That's Alicia Mae Emory's outfit!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, there are people in there who are not getting any happier!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. What are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I don't know, you guys figure it out, I got to put Emma down for a nap.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Hey Rach, while you're in there, throw something on Alicia Mae.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, what are we gonna say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh, we'll say that we were mugged! You can't get mad at someone who's been mugged!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, good, that's good, but you don't look like you were mugged!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Here (he tears off the pocket from Ross' shirt, and tears off everything below that as well.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey) HEY!
Monica's apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you hear something? (Chandlers stands up and goes to the door to look out from the peephole)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (peeping) They're out there!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh! Let me see! Oh God, I can't believe this! They're an hour late and they're just staying out there, talking!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (peeping) Everything is so distorted! Looks like Joey has a giant hand! Which says "Rangers" on it. They went to the game!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gasps) Oooh! They are in for a world of pain!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking through the peephole again) Ross' shirt is torn.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! They're late and they're sloppy!
[Cut to the hall]
Rachel comes out of her apartment}

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, Emma is napping... (then to Ross) what happened to your shirt?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I got mugged. And they stole my pocket.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We're just... we're trying to figure out an excuse. Hey! Ooh! How about this: We can say that Monica told us 5 o'clock, not 4 o'clock. That way we're right on time! (Others start to agree but she continues) OR... or, we can plant PCP in the apartment and call the cops on her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) That would be a good way to get rid of all the PCP we have lying around.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what, we just say that she said it was 5 o'clock. We'll just act casual. We're not late, we're right on time. (When she finishes talking, a note is pushed from under Monica's and Chandler's door, into the hall. Ross picks it up and reads it out loud)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Reading the note) We know you're out there. (Rachel gasps)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (whispering) Who do you think its from?
[time lapse - they are still in the hall]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God. This is bad. This is so bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, let's just go in there and face them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm not going in first. I bet that vein on Monica's forehead is popping like crazy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate that thing, it's like a... bolt of lightning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hey, I have an idea. Why don't we play rock-paper-scissors, and whoever loses goes in first. (they all agree) Ready? (they do the rock-paper-scissor thing with they hands and Rachel has paper, Phoebe and Ross both have rock, while Joey is doing a strange upward wiggling with his fingers. They all look a him confused).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling from ear to ear) Ah-haah! I win!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's fire. Beats everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, really? Does it beat water balloon? (She places her hand over his "fire" and mimics a bursting water balloon, thus putting the fire out).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! Well played, Phoebe Buffay, well played.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, enough, enough, come on. Let's just all go in at the same time.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Alright, okay. (Phoebe reaches for the door, and tries to open it but it's locked)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's locked.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha...? Oh sure, now they lock it, but when they're having sex on the couch, its like: "Come on in, my butt is surprisingly hairy".

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, come on... (starts to knock on the door) Alright, you guys. We're so sorry we're late. Please let us in, so we can have dinner together.
[cut to Monica and Chandler]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Everything's cold. The turkey's dried out and the... the stuffing is all soggy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, and there's a bowl of cranberry sauce that... (speaking lower to Monica) what happens to cranberry sauce?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Rolling her eyes) Nothing. It's fine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (relieved) Oh thank God!
[cut to the hall]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on you guys, we're sorry, alri...? Our subway broke down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking through the peephole) That's a lie, you went to the game, I can see Joey's hand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey, who is wearing a blue, 3 foot hand) FOR THE LOVE OF GOD TAKE IT OFF!! (takes it off his hand and throws it on the floor)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys, come on, it doesn't matter why we're late. We're all here now, please let us in so we can have some of your delicious turkey. (A slice of turkey on a piece of aluminum foil is slid under door)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I had a dream once about a fax machine that did that. (Ross picks it up)
[cut to Monica and Chandler]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's all the turkey you're gonna get.
[cut to the hall]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How are we gonna decide who gets this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (holds his fist up) WATER BALLOON! (he stuffs the slice in is mouth and gives the foil back to Ross)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are we gonna do? I'm starving.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Oh, I just remembered. We do have something to eat. Monica put something in our oven this morning.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah!
[cut to Monica and Chandler]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, you touch that and you will be sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys, I'd listen to her. The vein is bigger than I've ever seen it. (Monica looks at Chandler)
[cut to the hall]
Rachel comes out of the apartment holding a pot. Joey holds in anticipation and Rachel opens it

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh... OH MY GOD IT'S BRUSSELS SPROUTS. (they all look appalled)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's worse than no food.
[cut to Monica and Chandler]
Monica looks insulted

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: HA-HA! All you got was Monica's stinky Brussels sprouts!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Stinky?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please let me stay on this side of the door.
[cut to hall]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know... I still have my old key! (She goes in to get it and comes back with her keys) We can just unlock the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I don't know if that's such a good idea. They clearly don't want to be with us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what? I don't want to be with them either, but it's Thanksgiving and we should not want to be together, together. (Goes to unlock the door)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) Just get in there and make a face to face apology, you know? Look them in the eye. I know I can get them to forgive us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm telling ya... (his eyes open wide and he looks like his eyes are about to pop out. He stares at Ross like this without blinking) I can do it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Staring back, and then breaks off) (To Phoebe and Rachel) Yeah, he can do it!
Rachel finally manages to open the door, but the door chain is on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!
Joey sticks his head through the gap. Monica and Chandler are now sitting at the dinner table. There is another smaller table full of food standing in front of the front door
Monica and Chandler do not look amused by Joeys head in the door

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! It all looks so beautiful: the turkey, the stuffing...
Monica is now smiling

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The cranberries...?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Enough! A monkey could have made 'em!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (still with his door wedged in the door gap, now opens his eyes wide and stares at Monica and Chandler as he did with Ross earlier in the hall) Hey listen guys, we feel really terrible.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's doing that weird eye contact thing. Don't look at him, don't look at him! (They both look away)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on you guys, we want you to know we're (His eye widen even more) very very sorry. (Monica and Chandler are now covering their eyes with their hands) (then to the others in the hall) Right guys?
Ross head appears above Joey's, Rachel's below Joey's and Phoebe's at the bottom

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I feel terrible.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: So, so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) Now let's not ruin this day. You worked so hard. Let's move past this and try to have a nice meal all together, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The floating heads do make a good point.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, they do seem to feel pretty bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So bad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So bad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Gets up) Okay, okay. You two (to Phoebe and Rachel) go get the dessert. And I'll let you in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Dessert?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I asked you and Phoebe to pick up the pies. You did remember, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Pies, oh, we thought you said priiiize (goes to the hall and comes back with Emma's trophy in her hand). Here! (gives it to Monica).
Monica takes it and reads the label

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Grand Supreme Little Darling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Congratulatioooons!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! YOU FORGOT THE PIES? Well, I cannot believe this. You force me to make dinner, then you're an hour late and you forget the one little thing that I asked you to do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really girls, not cool.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you manheads aren't any better. You lied about going to the game. You knew it would make you late, and you still went anyway.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I'm getting a little tired of this okay? We said we're sorry. It's Thanksgiving for Pete's sakes! A day of forgiveness!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a day to be thankful.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross, mouthing) Don't make me come up there!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's too late for apologies.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine! Let's just go. I don't need your stupid dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That would be a lot more convincing if you weren't drooling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ewww, is that what that is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on you guys, let's just do our own Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I'll cook!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Let's go out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! You three have a nice Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The three of us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! You, Chan, and the vein!
Monica gasps and holds her forehead. Phoebe, Rachel and Ross pull back their heads

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ha!
When Joey tries to pull back his head, he notices he is stuck

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh! I'm stuck!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, that is not gonna work.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No seriously... I'm really wedged in here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll pull you through.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in agony) aaw-ahhh-aaahhh STOP! STOP! I'm worried about damaging my head.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A little late for that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, hurry up, you gotta do something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, well, this does not change anything. (to Chandler) Okay, we need to get something to grease the sides of his face.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhm, we've got turkey grease.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bring it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I just wanna say that I'm sorry I referred to the vein as a seperate person...
Chandler gives Monica a footlong "eye dropper" with the turkey grease in it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here you go!
She squirts some of the grease along Joey's face.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that smells good!
And he starts licking the grease which trickles down his face. Monica also squirts some on the other side of his face, and his tongue follows her movements.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, try it.
Joey tries to pull back at all his might.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It isn't working.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, we're gonna have to unscrew the chain.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well hurry, I can't feel my ears!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you ever feel your ears?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, where are your tools?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcastically) Oh, I left them on my bulldozer... I don't have tools!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I do, but Rachel borrowed them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I lent them to Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gave them to Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I left them at the park.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at Joey's butt) I'm finding it really hard not to mess with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've already stuffed a bunch of Brussel sprouts down his pants.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nice!
The phone rings in Chandler and Monica's apartment

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I have to get that. Now when I get back, I want you and your friends to be gone. Thanksgiving is over. The Vein has spoken.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's really starting to hurt.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, look, I'm gonna pull on the door and you guys push as hard as you can. Maybe we can get enough room to wiggle him out, okay? Okay, so PUSH!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Just a sec., we're kind in the middle of something here.
Rachel, Ross and Phoebe have their hands full and are stuffing all kinds of things down Joeys pants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! Stop putting things down my pants!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on guys, PUSH!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Come on!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: PUSH! PUSH! PUSH!

The chain breaks loose from the wall, and because Joey was pushing with all his might, he propells into the kitchen, towards the table with all the food. This table has wheels underneath it, and when Joey falls on this table, he rides into the living room, with all the food falling off, until finally Joey also falls off... Joey gets up quickly, a bit agitated, and acting as nothing happened. He is covered in food stains.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My cranberries!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at himself) Man, I've got food all over me.
He licks his fingers, liking it. He offers Chandler a taste.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Argh! I can't believe what you did. Monica's gonna kill you!
Monica enters from the spare bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) Look! Look! Look what the... Look what... Look what the floating heads did!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (very emotional) I don't care.
Chandler can't believe what he's hearing. He looks at Monica, then at the others, then back at Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was the adoption agency...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: WE'RE GETTING A BABY!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious? (they hug)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There's a pregnant woman in Ohio, and she picked us!
They all cheer and Rachel, Ross and Phoebe join in for a group hug. Joey also joins, but he stands back a bit, because he is all sticky of the food on him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so happy for you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This Thanksgiving kicks last Thanksgiving's ass!
They all cheer and hug again, but Joey's eating the food off his shirt
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Chandler and Monica's apartment. The table is set, and there's food on the tables again. They're all there, toasting.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: To Monica and Chandler... and that knocked up girl in Ohio.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm just so happy you guys are finally getting a kid.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. Have you considered pageanting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe they called, and we're actually getting a baby. (she kisses Chandler)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I know how you feel...
Monica and Chandler are looking curiously at Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure. I went through the exact same thing with Alicia Mae Emory... The waiting, the wandering... Then one day... I get that call from Toys "R" Us... She was in stock!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is the exact same thing.
THE END
